item_name: Sound + Voice Asset Creation (sonic logo, notifications, voice)
definition_origin: Audio assets — sonic logo (2-5s mnemonic), notification SFX, voice-over, ambient/music beds — comprise a brand's auditory identity. They exist because sound is processed faster than visuals (perception in <100ms), bypasses screen-attention, and persists across modalities (background, locked screen, audio-only contexts). In 2026 sonic branding has shifted from 'one logo' to 'cohesive sonic system' covering UI taps, voice prompts, marketing, and product moments derived from a single mnemonic.
core_purpose: At Norman's VISCERAL level, audio is the most direct emotion trigger — a 200ms tone can shift mood before a frame renders. At BEHAVIORAL level, distinct notification SFX teach users which alerts matter (urgency hierarchy via timbre, length, sharpness). At REFLECTIVE level, the sonic logo carries brand recognition and pride-of-association (Intel bong, Netflix tu-dum, McDonald's I'm Lovin' It). For wellness/meditation apps the entire experience is sonically led — visuals are often minimal because eyes are closed.
when_needed: MANDATORY for: meditation/wellness (Calm, Headspace, Finch, Insight Timer), gaming (every interaction), voice-first AI wrappers (Pi, Friend, Replika), audio learning (Duolingo, language apps), kids apps. STRONGLY ADVISED for: any consumer mobile with notifications or onboarding (subtle SFX = polish signal). OPTIONAL for: B2B dashboards, dev tools, internal tooling — though even these benefit from a 1-3 SFX palette (success ding, error buzz, completion chime). Marketing video always benefits from a sonic logo even if the app itself is silent.
format_options: MP3 (universal, 128-192kbps for SFX is fine, smaller files; lossy — fine for short SFX, avoid for music masters). M4A/AAC (better quality at same bitrate than MP3, native iOS, slightly larger). WAV (uncompressed master archive; do not ship to mobile — 10x size). OGG Vorbis (Android-native, smaller than MP3 at same quality, no iOS native support). OPUS (best modern codec, used by Discord/WhatsApp; supported in modern browsers and React Native expo-av; 30-50% smaller than MP3 at same quality). CAF (Apple's container, used for system sounds — convert via afconvert). AHAP (Apple Haptic Audio Pattern — JSON spec describing tap/buzz patterns; pairs with audio for haptic-audio mini-compositions). For sonic logo: ship MP3 + M4A fallback. For notifications: short MP3 < 50KB, mono, normalized -3dB. For meditation music: streaming OPUS/AAC at 96-128kbps stereo. For voice-over: AAC/M4A at 64kbps mono (speech compresses heavily).
file_size_performance: Notification SFX target <30KB, <500ms duration, mono, -3dB peak normalize. Sonic logo <100KB, 2-5s, stereo. Voice-over per minute ~500KB at 64kbps mono AAC. Music beds stream don't bundle — use expo-av or react-native-track-player streaming from CDN. Pre-load critical SFX (success/error) at app start; lazy-load others. Avoid bundling >2MB of audio in app binary; offload to CDN with first-launch fetch.
free_tools: ElevenLabs Sound Effects (text-to-SFX, free tier ~60 SFX/mo with 'elevenlabs.io' attribution required for free; 22 sec max per generation; great for whoosh/ding/click/explosion). Suno AI (text-to-music with vocals, free tier 50 credits/day = ~10 songs at 2min, NON-COMMERCIAL on free tier — must upgrade to $10/mo Pro for commercial rights). Stable Audio Open (open-source, run locally on GPU, up to 47s, NON-COMMERCIAL license — research/prototype only). Bfxr / bfxr2 (browser, retro 8-bit/chiptune SFX, completely free, MIT — perfect for game-like UI feedback). ChipTone by SFBGames (free, cross-platform, includes sampler/vocoder/sequencer; 8-bit and modern SFX). jsfxr (web sfxr port). Audacity (free DAW, all platforms — trim, normalize, fade, export; the universal editor). ElevenLabs Voice (free tier 10k chars/mo, 3 custom voices, attribution required; 32+ languages with v3 expressive control). Microsoft Edge TTS via edge-tts Python lib (FREE, 322 voices, 74 languages, no API key — MIT/open source wrapper of Edge's cloud TTS). Pixabay Music (~30k tracks, royalty-free, NO attribution required, COMMERCIAL OK). Free Music Archive (~180k tracks, per-track Creative Commons license — read each one). Freesound.org (CC-BY/CC0 SFX library, attribution required for CC-BY tracks). YouTube Audio Library (free, attribution-free, commercial OK, large library). Uppbeat (free tier with attribution). META Haptics Studio (free Mac/iOS app for AHAP design from audio).
paid_tools: STARTER tier ($10-15/mo): Suno Pro $10/mo (500 songs/mo, commercial rights, no watermark, Song Editor) — best value for any app needing custom music. ElevenLabs Starter $5/mo (30k chars TTS, commercial rights, voice cloning). PRO tier ($20-30/mo): ElevenLabs Creator $22/mo (100k chars, 192kbps, professional voice clones, Sound Effects unlimited). Krisp $12/mo (noise removal for voice recording). ENTERPRISE ($50+/mo): Splice $13-50/mo (royalty-free sample library, stems). AIVA $33/mo (composer-grade music). Loudly $20/mo. DO NOT PAY for sonic-branding agency ($5-50k/logo) until the app has revenue — DIY with Suno + ElevenLabs gets you 80% of the way for $0-10/mo.
gemini_pro_workflow: Gemini Pro itself does not generate audio directly, but is exceptional for audio asset PLANNING and PROMPT ENGINEERING. (1) Brand-to-sound translation: paste brand archetype + emotional spec into Gemini and ask 'translate this brand into 5 sound design directions with reference tracks, BPM, instrumentation, and texture'. (2) Sonic logo brief: 'Generate a 200-word sonic logo brief for [brand]: 3-second mnemonic, BPM range, instruments, emotional arc, brand attribute mapping, 3 reference logos for inspiration'. (3) Suno prompt construction: feed the brand brief and ask 'write 5 Suno prompts (instrumental, 30s) that capture this brief; include genre, BPM, instruments, mood, and structural notes'. (4) ElevenLabs SFX prompts: 'generate 10 ElevenLabs Sound Effects prompts for app notifications (success, error, like, message, achievement, level-up, gentle nudge, alarm, dismiss, swoosh) — each <15 words, evocative, reference sound design vocabulary'. (5) Voice-script writing: Gemini Pro writes warm onboarding scripts and emotional copy then you pipe into ElevenLabs. (6) NotebookLM audio overview: feed your design docs and let it generate a podcast-style discussion to discover unstated emotional notes. (7) Use Gemini's long context to maintain a sonic-style guide doc that all future prompts reference for consistency.
workflow_steps: Step 1 — Brand audio brief (30 min in Gemini Pro): from emotional_spec.md, derive 5 sound directions, BPM range, instrumentation, mood adjectives, reference logos. Step 2 — Sonic logo draft (1-2 hr): write 3-5 Suno prompts with Gemini's help; generate 20-30 candidates on Suno free tier; pick top 3 favorites. Step 3 — Sonic logo refinement (1 hr): trim to 2-4s in Audacity, fade in/out, normalize -3dB, export MP3 + M4A. Stem-extract if needed via Suno Pro Studio. Step 4 — Notification SFX palette (1-2 hr): list 6-10 notification types; for each generate via ElevenLabs SFX (organic) OR ChipTone/Bfxr (retro/playful) OR Suno (musical chimes); ensure tonal coherence (same key family, decay envelope). Step 5 — Audio cleanup (1 hr): batch-process in Audacity — high-pass at 80Hz, normalize, fade, export MP3 mono < 30KB. Step 6 — Voice-over (1 hr per 5 min of audio): write script in Gemini Pro, generate via ElevenLabs (premium) or edge-tts (free); pick one persistent voice across app. Step 7 — Music beds (2-3 hr): for meditation/wellness sections generate via Suno or pull from Pixabay/FMA; verify license; store in CDN; stream don't bundle. Step 8 — Haptic pairs (1 hr per critical SFX): in META Haptics Studio import audio, auto-generate AHAP, refine sharpness/intensity. Step 9 — Integrate (1 hr): drop into apps/mobile/assets/audio/, wire up via expo-av or react-native-sound. Step 10 — Audit (30 min): listen at headphones AND phone speakers AND in noisy environment; verify all sounds <30KB, normalized.
ai_prompt_templates: Sonic logo via Suno: 'Cinematic sonic logo, {ARCHETYPE_ADJECTIVES}, {INSTRUMENTS}, BPM {BPM}, 3 seconds, fade-in fade-out, no vocals, ends on resolved chord, mood: {EMOTIONAL_PROMISE}, reference: {REFERENCE_BRANDS}'. Notification SFX via ElevenLabs: 'A {DURATION_MS}ms {ADJECTIVE} {SOUND_TYPE} for an app notification, {TIMBRE} timbre, {DECAY} decay, mono, no reverb tail, designed to feel {EMOTIONAL_TONE}, modern UI sound design'. Meditation bed via Suno: 'Ambient meditation bed, no melody, drone-based, 432Hz tuning, {INSTRUMENT} pads, slow attack, 60 BPM, 3 minutes, no rhythmic percussion, reference: Brian Eno Music for Airports, no vocals'. Voice-over via ElevenLabs: 'Voice: {VOICE_ID} (warm/calm/{ARCHETYPE}). Style: {WHISPER|CONVERSATIONAL|NARRATION}. Stability 0.5, similarity 0.75. Script: {SCRIPT}'. Brand-to-sound translation via Gemini: 'I have a {ARCHETYPE} brand with the emotional promise of {PROMISE}. Translate this into a sonic identity spec: BPM range, scale/mode, primary instruments, texture, attack/decay character, reverb tail, brightness, energy curve. Then write 3 Suno prompts and 5 ElevenLabs SFX prompts that match.'
claude_code_integration: Drop assets into apps/mobile/assets/audio/{logo,sfx,voice,music}/ with kebab-case names (sonic-logo.mp3, sfx-success.mp3, voice-onboard-welcome.m4a). For Expo projects, install expo-av (or its successor expo-audio in Expo SDK 52+) and react-native-haptic-feedback. Have Claude Code generate a useAppSounds hook that pre-loads critical SFX with Audio.Sound.createAsync and exposes play(name) — including reduced-motion/silent-mode bypass. For dotLottie + audio sync use a single timestamp source and trigger sound at frame X via onAnimationFinish. Add a metro.config.js asset extension whitelist for .mp3, .m4a, .opus. For build-time audio optimization, add a npm script: 'audio:optimize': 'find assets/audio -name *.mp3 -exec ffmpeg -i {} -b:a 96k -ac 1 -ar 44100 {}.opt.mp3 \;'. Wire AHAP files via react-native-haptic-feedback or a custom Swift bridge.
emotional_design_integration: CAREGIVER (Calm, Headspace's calmer surfaces, Finch): warm pads, piano notes, gentle ambience, slow attack, long decay, 60-80 BPM, hushed voice-over, no sharp transients. MAGICIAN (Apple, AI products like Pi): luminous synth swells, evolving textures, glassy bell tones, single sparkle hit, mystery; voice intimate, slightly processed. JESTER (Duolingo, Headspace's marketing surfaces): bright marimba/xylophone, ascending arpeggios, bouncy 110-120 BPM, vocal exclamations, playful 8-bit references; voice energetic, exaggerated warmth. CREATOR (Notion, Figma): minimal taps, refined click textures, subtle whoosh, no melody, restrained; voice articulate, clear, neutral-warm. HERO (Nike, Strava): big drums, anthemic chord stabs, rising triplets, bold downbeats, percussive transients; voice declarative, masculine-pitched-down, motivating. SAGE (Wikipedia, Bloomberg): subtle acoustic timbres, neutral interface clicks, almost no notification SFX, restrained voice-over, broadcast-quality reading. OUTLAW (Reddit, Telegram, Liquid Death): distorted noise hits, broken textures, off-grid timing, lo-fi character; voice gritty, irreverent. RULER (LVMH apps, banking premium tier): orchestral stabs, gold-toned bells, deep cellos, restraint and authority; voice deep, measured, deliberate. Across all archetypes Norman's VISCERAL = sonic logo first impression; BEHAVIORAL = SFX teach hierarchy and feedback; REFLECTIVE = consistent timbral identity = brand pride.
specific_examples: Headspace (Jester-Caregiver hybrid) — bright orange visual identity paired with breathing-paced ambient beds, soft chime SFX for completion, warm Andy Puddicombe voice-over that became a brand asset itself. Calm (Caregiver) — Tamara Levitt's voice is the brand more than the logo; nature-recording-driven SFX (rain on leaves, crackling fire), no clinical alerts. Duolingo (Jester) — TONS of SFX (correct ding, streak fire, owl voice clips, level-up fanfare); each lesson is a sonic mini-game; mascot voice clips reinforce character. Apple (Magician-Ruler) — minimal but iconic: 'tu-tu-rock' Mac startup, lock click, payment chime; consistent timbral DNA across decades. Slack (Caregiver-Creator) — knock-brush, ding, hummingbird; designed with sound designer Sander van Dijck to be 'gentle but distinct in noisy office'. Netflix tu-dum (Magician) — 0.8s ascending power chord, instantly recognizable. References: https://www.audiobrain.com/sonic-logos , https://www.musicgrid.com/blog/sonic-branding-global-examples , https://standards.site/case-studies/headspace/ , https://blakecrosley.com/guides/design/headspace .
anti_ai_slop_traps: AI-music slop tells: Suno default 'epic cinematic trailer' fanfare (over-orchestrated brass + 808s + female 'oohs'); generic lo-fi-hip-hop with vinyl crackle; saccharine major-7 piano with autotuned 'mmm' vocals; recognizable Suno reverb signature on every stem. AI-SFX slop tells: ElevenLabs default whoosh sounds identical across thousands of YouTube shorts; AI-generated 'success ding' is invariably a major-third bell at 880Hz with too-long reverb tail. Voice slop tells: ElevenLabs default 'Adam' or 'Bella' voice now ubiquitous; over-emphatic AI prosody on questions; mid-sentence breath-stutter artifacts. COUNTERS: (1) Always edit AI output in Audacity — trim 20-30% of length, dry the reverb, EQ-cut 2-5kHz where AI smearing lives. (2) Layer AI + human — record a single tap on your desk + AI sound, mix at 30/70. (3) Use ChipTone/Bfxr for SFX — 8-bit synthesis sounds intentional and is impossible to AI-fake. (4) Generate 10x what you need and pick weirdest-best, not most-polished. (5) For voice, custom-clone a 30-second sample of yourself in ElevenLabs rather than picking default voices. (6) Run all AI outputs through analog tape emulation (free Chow Tape Model VST) for grit. (7) Avoid Suno's 'cinematic' or 'epic' prompts — use specific reference tracks instead.
common_pitfalls: (1) Volume mismatch — AI tools output at varying dB; always normalize to -3dB peak / -16 LUFS for consistency. (2) Stereo SFX wasting bytes — notification SFX should be mono, 99% of users hear them on one phone speaker. (3) Reverb tails that bleed into next sound — trim ruthlessly; SFX should be <500ms total. (4) Forgetting to test on phone speaker — bass-heavy SFX disappear on tinny mobile speakers. (5) Bundling huge MP3s in app binary — stream music, lazy-load voice; cache after first play. (6) Auto-playing audio without user gesture — iOS/Android both block this; need user tap to initialize audio session. (7) Ignoring silent mode / Do Not Disturb — respect system settings; check Audio.setAudioModeAsync. (8) No fallback when audio fails — never gate functionality on audio loading. (9) Inconsistent voice/SFX across surfaces — define a small palette (1 voice, 6-8 SFX, 1 sonic logo) and lock it. (10) Licensing roulette — Suno free tier is NON-COMMERCIAL; always verify license before shipping.
accessibility_notes: Always pair audio cues with visual + haptic redundancy (color change + vibration + sound) — never audio-only signal for status. Provide a system setting to disable all sounds (most users keep phones silent anyway). Honor system-level reduce-motion AND mute switch — use Audio.setIsEnabledAsync(false) when system is silent. Voice-over: include written transcripts of any narrated content; pair with VoiceOver/TalkBack-friendly alt text on the play button. For meditation: subtitle/caption ambient tracks with visual breathing indicator. Avoid sudden loud transients (>-6dB jumps) which can hurt hearing-aid users.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile: bundle small SFX in app, stream music; respect mute switch; audio session must be configured for background playback (expo-av setAudioModeAsync); haptics available (Core Haptics on iOS, VibrationEffect on Android). Web: autoplay blocked until user gesture; use Web Audio API for low-latency SFX (HTMLAudio has 100-300ms delay); preload via <audio preload='auto'>; no haptics except limited Vibration API on Android Chrome.
sources: [ElevenLabs Sound Effects](https://elevenlabs.io/sound-effects) — text-to-SFX free tier and pricing 2026. [Suno Pricing 2026](https://suno.com/pricing) — free 50 credits/day, $10 Pro for commercial. [Stable Audio Open on Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/stabilityai/stable-audio-open-1.0) — open weights, non-commercial license. [ChipTone by SFBGames](https://sfbgames.itch.io/chiptone) and [Bfxr](https://www.bfxr.net/) — free retro SFX generators. [edge-tts on GitHub](https://github.com/rany2/edge-tts) — free Microsoft Edge TTS Python wrapper. [Pixabay Music](https://pixabay.com/music/) — royalty-free, no attribution required. [Free Music Archive](https://freemusicarchive.org/) — 180k CC-licensed tracks. [Freesound.org](https://www.freesound.org/) — collaborative CC SFX library. [Apple WWDC21 Practice Audio Haptic Design](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2021/10278/) — AHAP and Core Haptics. [Stephen Arnold Music: 2026 Sonic Branding Trends](https://stephenarnoldmusic.com/the-state-of-sonic-2026-trends-in-sonic-branding/) — current sonic branding direction. [Audiobrain Sonic Logos](https://www.audiobrain.com/sonic-logos) — agency case studies. [Headspace Brand Standards](https://standards.site/case-studies/headspace/) — sound + visual integration.
